name: Accordion
description: A set of collapsible panels with headings for progressive content disclosure. Built on Base UI Accordion with StyleX styling.

Root.description: Container that groups all accordion items.

value.description: Controlled value of expanded items. Each value corresponds to an Item's value prop.

defaultValue.description: Initial expanded items for uncontrolled mode.

onValueChange.description: Callback fired when items expand or collapse.

multiple.description: Allow multiple panels to be open simultaneously.

disabled.description: Disable all accordion items. Can also be set per-item.

dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the accordion is disabled.

Item.description: Groups a header with its collapsible panel.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the item's panel is expanded.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the item is disabled.

Header.description: Heading wrapper for accessibility. Renders a heading element around the trigger.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the parent item's panel is expanded.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the parent item is disabled.

Trigger.description: Button that toggles the panel open and closed. Includes a built-in chevron icon that rotates on open.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the parent item's panel is expanded.
dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the parent item is disabled.

Panel.description: Collapsible content area with animated height transition. Uses keepMounted internally for smooth close animation.

keepMounted.description: Whether to keep the element in the DOM while closed. Defaults to true for animation support.

dataAttributes.data-open: Present when the panel is expanded.
dataAttributes.data-starting-style: Present when the panel is animating open.
dataAttributes.data-ending-style: Present when the panel is animating closed.

cssVariables.--accordion-panel-height: The panel's content height, set by Base UI. Used for expand/collapse animation.

cssRequirements.description: Panel height animation requires global CSS rules inside @layer priority1. StyleX cannot target data-attribute selectors, so these rules must live in the consumer's global stylesheet.

intents.reasoning: Accordion is the primary component for organizing content into collapsible sections.
Accordion provides progressive disclosure — content is hidden by default and revealed on demand.
intents.composition:
<Accordion.Root>
  <Accordion.Item value="details">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Show details</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Hidden content revealed on click</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

intents.reasoning: FAQ lists are a classic accordion pattern — each question is a trigger, each answer is a panel.
intents.composition:
<Accordion.Root>
  <Accordion.Item value="q1">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>How do I reset my password?</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Visit the settings page and click "Reset Password".</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

avoidWhen.scenario: Switching between views or content panels
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Tabs instead. Accordion reveals content in place; Tabs switch between distinct views.

avoidWhen.scenario: A single collapsible section with no siblings
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Collapsible directly. Accordion is for groups of related collapsible sections.
Accordion doesn't communicate sequential progress or completion state.

examples.name: basic
examples.code:
<Accordion.Root>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-1">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section 1</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Content for section 1</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-2">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section 2</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Content for section 2</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

examples.name: multiple
examples.code:
<Accordion.Root multiple>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-1">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section 1</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Content for section 1</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-2">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section 2</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>Content for section 2</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

examples.name: disabled
examples.code:
<Accordion.Root>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-1">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Enabled</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>This section works</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-2" disabled>
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Disabled</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>This section is disabled</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

examples.name: default-expanded
examples.description: Accordion with a panel open by default
examples.code:
<Accordion.Root defaultValue={["item-1"]}>
  <Accordion.Item value="item-1">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Open by default</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>This panel starts open</Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>
